title: Error Lane Runbook v1
description: Defines error classification, recovery procedures, and escalation rules for workflow_error and platform_error lanes.

error_classification.description: Rules for classifying errors into workflow_error or platform_error.

rules.condition: Error originates from tool/API failure (timeout, connection reset, service unavailable)
rules.rationale: Infrastructure issue, not workflow logic fault.

rules.condition: Error originates from invalid artifact, schema violation, or boundary violation
Error originates from resource exhaustion (disk, memory, rate limit)
Error originates from verification failure (check returned fail or blocked)
rules.rationale: Verification found a defect in workflow output.

rules.condition: Error type is ambiguous or unknown
rules.rationale: Fail-closed: treat unknown errors as platform errors to trigger bounded recovery.

workflow_error_handling.description: Handling for errors classified as workflow_error.
procedure: 1. Log error with correlation_id, source_state, error_detail.
3. IF under retry cap: route to previous active state (researching, planning, or building) via resume_policy.
4. IF retry cap exceeded: route to escalation.
5. Increment retry counter for this workflow.

platform_error_handling.description: Handling for errors classified as platform_error.
procedure: 1. Log error with correlation_id, source_state, error_detail, platform_context.
2. Record previous_state for resume after recovery.
4. Automatically route to blocked_platform (PlatformRecovery takes ownership).

recovery_runbook.description: Bounded recovery procedures executed by PlatformRecovery in blocked_platform state.

actions.name: retry_operation
actions.description: Retry the failed operation with exponential backoff.

actions.name: reconnect
actions.description: Tear down and re-establish connection to the failed service.

actions.name: clear_cache
actions.description: Clear local caches that may contain stale or corrupted data.

action_selection: 1. Match error type to applicable_when tags.
2. Execute matching actions in order (RA-001 first, then RA-002, etc.).
3. After each action, run health verification.
4. IF health passes: signal recovery success.
5. IF all applicable actions exhausted without health pass: signal recovery failure.
exit_conditions: Health verification passes after a recovery action

health_verification.description: Independent health check required before resume signal.

checks.name: service_connectivity
checks.description: Verify the previously-failed service responds to a health probe.

checks.name: state_consistency
checks.description: Verify workflow state has not been corrupted during the error/recovery cycle.

checks.name: artifact_integrity
checks.description: Verify all artifacts produced before the error are still accessible and valid.

health_verification.fail_action: Continue recovery actions or escalate if exhausted

escalation_from_error.description: When recovery or retry is exhausted, escalation engages the Operator to present status to the user.
